pubmed_links: [Scala et al. (2025) Bi-allelic variants in the ribosomal protein RPS6KC1 cause a complex neurodevelopmental disorder](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/41130203/)
summary: Bi-allelic loss-of-function variants in RPS6KC1 were recently identified in 13 individuals across 8 families, causing a neurodevelopmental disorder with delay, hypotonia, spastic paraplegia, and white matter abnormalities through disruption of ribosomal protein synthesis and the mTOR/PI3K pathway. (novel candidate)
